revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '天神社（てんじんしゃ）は福岡県糸島市神在にある天照大神を祀る神社。 神功皇后が三韓征伐へ向かう途中、天神山...'
revision.text: 天神社（てんじんしゃ）は福岡県糸島市神在にある天照大神を祀る神社。 神功皇后が三韓征伐へ向かう途中、天神山と鷹ヶ峰(宮地嶽)の間を霞がたなびく姿に「此の処には神が有ます」と瑞祥を得た由縁の地である天神山に天照大神を祀る。元は伊勢神宮で奉仕をしていた神主が自家で祀っていた邸内社であったが、現在の地に遷座。

{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[糸島市]]神在１１８９番１９ | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = 33 |緯度秒 = 543588 | 経度度 = |経度分 =130 |経度秒 = 169516 |祭神 = [[天照大神]] |社格 = [[無格社]] }}
[[天神社]]（てんじんしゃ）は[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]神在にある[[天照大神]]を祀る[[神社]]。 [[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]へ向かう途中、天神山と鷹ヶ峰(宮地嶽)の間を霞がたなびく姿に「此の処には神が有ます」と瑞祥を得た由縁の地、天神山に[[天照大神]]を祀る。 はじめ[[伊勢神宮]]の元[[神職]]の邸内社であったが、平成の御代に現在の地に遷座した。

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | Location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | Deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | Shrine Rank = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Tenjinja (Itoshima)-qq]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenjin Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]]

[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qqqqqq]] <!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qqqqqq]] <!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] <!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] <!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] <!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] <!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] <!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)--> {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
According to legend, when [[Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}}
* {{ill|Tenjinja (Akashi)|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)--> - Located in {{ill|Akashi|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->, Hyogo Prefecture.
== Shikoku Region == * {{ill|Tokushima Bizen Tenjinja|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)--> - Located in {{ill|Bizencho|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->, {{ill|Tokushima|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->, Tokushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima)|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)--> - Located in Kitamiya, Tokushima. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima-Iritacho)|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)--> - Located in Irita, Tokushima.
== Kyushu Region == * - Located in Kamiaru, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en}}
<!--Tenjinja (Itoshima)-->, Fukuoka Prefecture.
{{Aimai]]

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].

According to legend, when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.

Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].

According to legend, when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.

Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (糸島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119506]] Wikidata name: Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Notability|date=April 2015}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Itoshima, Fukuoka}} | location = 3-1189-19 Kamiari Nishi, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan 819-1148 | ISO = JP-40 | Latitude = 33 | Lat_min = 32 | Lat_sec = 36.9 | Longitude = 130 | Lon_min = 10 | Lon_sec = 10.3 | Label Position = right | Map = Japan Fukuoka # Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | type = [[Mukakusha]] (Unranked Shrine) | location = {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.54358|N|130.169539|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
{{nihongo|Tenjinsha|天神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
According to legend, when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}}

{{nihongo|Tenjinsha|天神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
According to legend, when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was on her way to the [[Conquest of the Three Han]], she saw mist drifting between *Tenjinyama* (Tenjin Mountain) and *Takagamine* (Miyajidake) and interpreted it as an auspicious sign, exclaiming, "There is a divine presence here." Because of this, *Amaterasu Ōmikami* was enshrined on Tenjin Mountain.
Originally, the shrine was a private shrine (*tainai-sha*) of a former [[Ise Grand Shrine]] priest's residence. However, in the Heisei era, it was relocated to its current site.
{{Shinto-stub}}
